worth_it_explanation: Advantage+ Audience changes who sees the ads, so a setup that treats the intended audience as a suggestion when it should be a hard control can spend on people the advertiser meant to exclude, and a setup that locks targeting too tightly can starve the system of the reach it needs to find converters. The check is fast. The audience section of each ad set shows whether detailed targeting is acting as a control or a suggestion and whether the protected controls such as minimum age, location, and exclusions are set. The fix is a configuration change in the ad set, not a campaign rebuild, and it takes effect on the next delivery cycle.
intro: Advantage+ Audience lets Meta find the people most likely to convert rather than holding delivery to a fixed audience definition. Any custom audiences, lookalikes, and detailed targeting entered act as an audience suggestion that Meta can expand past, while a separate set of audience controls, such as minimum age, location, language, and exclusions, stays fixed and is never expanded. These checks confirm that the intended audience is treated as a control where it has to be, that the protected controls are set, and that broad targeting is used where it would perform rather than left off by habit.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ Audience treats the intended audience as a suggestion when it should be a control
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the intended audience is entered as a suggestion rather than a control, Meta can deliver beyond it to anyone it predicts will convert, so spend reaches people the advertiser meant to keep out. On a regulated or niche offer, that drift wastes budget and can surface the wrong creative to the wrong audience.

educate.base:
Advantage+ Audience splits what an advertiser enters into two kinds of input. Custom audiences, lookalikes, and detailed targeting act as an audience suggestion, which is a starting point Meta can deliver beyond when it predicts better performance elsewhere. A separate set of audience controls, such as minimum age, location, language, and exclusions, acts as a hard boundary that Meta never expands past.

The distinction matters because an audience entered only as a suggestion is not a fence. If a brand sells a product meant for one age band or one region and enters that as detailed targeting, Meta can still deliver to people outside it. The intended audience held the line in older audience setups, so a setting that reads as targeting in the ad set can give a false sense that delivery is constrained when it is not.

Whether a suggestion is the right choice depends on the offer. For a broad consumer product, letting Meta deliver past the suggestion is the point of Advantage+ Audience and usually helps. For an offer with a real eligibility boundary, the boundary belongs in the audience controls, where it is enforced, rather than in detailed targeting, where it is only a hint.

A few signs point to a boundary placed in the wrong layer:
  • A product with a genuine age or region limit has that limit in detailed targeting rather than in minimum age or location controls
  • The ad set's audience section shows the intended audience under suggestions with no matching control set
  • Delivery reports show conversions or reach well outside the audience the advertiser believes is targeted
You may want to confirm, for each ad set, that any hard eligibility boundary sits in the audience controls rather than in the suggestion layer. A boundary that has to hold belongs where Meta cannot expand past it.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, a suggestion-only setup is often fine because the goal is to find buyers wherever they are, and Advantage+ Audience is built for that reach. The exception is a catalog with shipping or legal limits, for example alcohol or age-restricted goods. You may want to confirm those limits sit in the minimum age and location controls rather than in detailed targeting, so delivery cannot reach a region the store cannot ship to or a buyer it cannot legally sell to.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, a suggestion the system can expand past can pull in leads outside the intended profile, which raises volume but lowers lead quality. If the offer has a firm boundary, for example a service available in set regions only, you may want to place that boundary in the location controls. Detailed targeting describing the ideal lead can stay a suggestion. Narrowing it too hard can starve a lead campaign of the reach it needs.

learn_more.source_title: About Audience controls and Audience suggestions in Advantage+ audience

investigate.intro: Open each active ad set and confirm whether the intended audience is entered as an audience control or only as a suggestion, then check that any hard eligibility boundary sits in the controls.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Ads Manager, select an active campaign, and open one of its ad sets.
In the audience section, read whether the campaign uses Advantage+ Audience and which entries appear under audience controls versus audience suggestions.
For any product or service with a real age or region boundary, note whether that boundary is set as minimum age or location under controls, or only described in detailed targeting under suggestions.
Repeat across ad sets, recording any where a hard boundary lives in the suggestion layer rather than the control layer.

meta_ads_reporting.label: Ads Manager reporting
steps: In Ads Manager, open the campaign's breakdown by age or by region for the ad sets in question.
Compare the delivered age and region against the audience the advertiser believes is targeted.
Note any ad set delivering meaningful reach or conversions outside the intended boundary, which confirms the boundary is acting as a suggestion rather than a control.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per ad set, whether any hard eligibility boundary is set as an audience control and whether delivery reporting shows reach outside that boundary. An ad set with no hard boundary needed, or with the boundary correctly placed in the controls, passes. An ad set whose boundary lives only in the suggestion layer, confirmed by delivery outside it, is a fail recorded with the ad set name and the boundary at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record, for each ad set above, whether a hard boundary is set as an audience control and whether delivery reached outside it to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that some of {{site_name}}'s Advantage+ Audience ad sets place a hard eligibility boundary in the suggestion layer rather than in the audience controls. Detailed targeting acts as a suggestion Meta can deliver beyond, so a boundary entered there does not hold, and delivery reached audiences the account meant to exclude. {{voice_recommend}} moving any firm age or region limit into the minimum age and location controls, where Meta cannot expand past it, and leaving the descriptive audience as a suggestion only where broader reach is acceptable. After the change, review the age and region delivery breakdowns to confirm reach stays inside the intended boundary.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ Audience controls for minimum age, location, and exclusions are left unset
audit_checks.impact_rationale: The audience controls are the only inputs Advantage+ Audience treats as fixed boundaries. Leaving minimum age, location, language, and exclusion lists unset lets Meta deliver to existing customers, employees, or ineligible audiences, which spends budget on people the advertiser would never have chosen to reach.

educate.base:
Audience controls are the part of an Advantage+ Audience setup that Meta treats as fixed. Minimum age, location, language, and audience exclusions act as boundaries the system never expands past, in contrast with the suggestion layer it can deliver beyond. They are the enforcement layer of the setup.

Exclusions deserve close attention because Advantage+ Audience reaches new people by design, and without an exclusion list it can deliver to people the advertiser already has. An account running prospecting without excluding recent purchasers or current customers pays prospecting prices to show ads to an audience it already converted. The same applies to internal audiences such as employees, who can absorb impressions and skew the early signal the campaign learns from.

Minimum age and location have a second role beyond performance. For age-restricted or region-restricted offers, they are how the account stays inside what it is allowed to advertise since detailed targeting cannot enforce that. Language can matter where an offer is served in one language and reach in others wastes spend.

A few controls are worth confirming on every prospecting ad set:
  • Exclusions for recent purchasers, current customers, and any internal audience such as employees
  • Minimum age set to match the offer's eligibility, not left at the default
  • Location set to the regions the offer actually serves or ships to
You may want to confirm these controls are populated rather than assuming the defaults are safe. An empty exclusion field is not a neutral choice in a system built to expand reach.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, the highest-value control is usually a customer exclusion. You may want to confirm a recent-purchaser or all-customers custom audience is set as an exclusion on prospecting ad sets, so the catalog is not advertised at prospecting cost to people who just bought. For age-restricted or shipping-restricted goods, confirm minimum age and location match the catalog's real limits.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, the exclusion that matters most is a list of people who already converted, for example a custom audience of existing leads or closed customers. Without it, Advantage+ Audience can keep serving the lead form to people already in the funnel. You may want to confirm that exclusion is in place, along with a location control matching the regions the service covers.

learn_more.source_title: Create a Campaign Using Advantage+ Audience
About Audience controls and Audience suggestions in Advantage+ audience

investigate.intro: Open each prospecting ad set and confirm whether the audience controls for exclusions, minimum age, and location are populated to match the offer.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Ads Manager, select a prospecting campaign, and open one of its ad sets.
In the audience section, expand the audience controls and read whether minimum age, location, and language are set to match the offer.
Check the exclusions field for a customer or recent-purchaser audience and for any internal audience such as employees.
Repeat across prospecting ad sets, recording any with an empty exclusion field or a default minimum age or location that does not match the offer.

meta_business_settings.label: Business Settings
steps: In Meta Business Suite, open Business Settings and review the custom audiences available to the account, such as a customer list or a recent-purchaser audience built from a dataset.

Confirm an appropriate exclusion audience exists. If none does, the ad set cannot exclude existing customers regardless of the control setting, which points to a missing audience rather than a missing toggle.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per prospecting ad set, whether an exclusion audience is set and whether minimum age and location match the offer. An ad set with the relevant exclusions in place and controls matching the offer passes. An ad set with an empty exclusion field, or a minimum age or location left at a default that does not fit the offer, is a fail recorded with the ad set name and the control at fault.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record, for each prospecting ad set above, whether exclusions are set and whether minimum age and location match the offer to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that some of {{site_name}}'s Advantage+ Audience ad sets leave the audience controls unset, with empty exclusion fields or default age and location settings. Audience controls are the only inputs Meta treats as fixed, so without them the system can deliver to existing customers, internal audiences, or ineligible regions, spending prospecting budget on people the account would not have chosen. {{voice_recommend}} adding exclusions for recent purchasers, current customers, and internal audiences, and setting minimum age and location to match each offer's real eligibility. After the change, confirm the exclusion audiences exist in Business Settings and that each prospecting ad set references them.

audit_checks.title: Advantage+ Audience is turned off where broad targeting plus a strong signal would perform better
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An account that pins delivery to a narrow saved audience out of habit can hold the system below the reach it needs to find converters, especially once a Conversions API signal is feeding it. The cost is opportunity rather than waste, which is why it scores lower, but it can cap scale on campaigns that could grow.

educate.base:
Advantage+ Audience pairs broad reach with Meta's prediction of who will convert. Rather than holding delivery to a fixed saved audience, it starts from any suggestion entered and finds converters across a wider pool. Its accuracy depends on the conversion signal it learns from, so it works best when a clean signal is reaching Meta from the pixel and the Conversions API (CAPI).

Turning the feature off and pinning delivery to a narrow saved audience can leave reach on the table. A tightly defined audience caps the pool the system can draw from, so even with a strong signal it cannot find converters it is not allowed to reach. On a brand-new account with no conversion history, narrow targeting can be a reasonable starting point. On an account already sending steady conversions through CAPI, the same narrow setup often holds the system below where it could perform.

The decision is not automatic since broad targeting helps most when the signal is reliable and the offer has wide appeal. A niche offer or a weak signal can make a defined audience the better call. The point of the check is to confirm the choice was deliberate rather than a default carried over from older audience setups.

A few conditions suggest broad targeting is worth testing:
  • The account sends steady conversions through the pixel and the Conversions API, giving Meta a reliable signal to optimize against
  • Delivery is pinned to a narrow saved audience that limits reach without a clear performance reason
  • The offer has broad appeal rather than a tight eligibility boundary
You may want to consider testing Advantage+ Audience against the current narrow setup where these conditions hold, rather than assuming the existing audience is optimal.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store with a steady purchase signal feeding the Conversions API, broad targeting through Advantage+ Audience usually has enough data to find buyers across a wide pool. You may want to test it against the current saved-audience setup on a prospecting campaign and compare cost per purchase, rather than keeping a narrow audience by default.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, broad targeting can raise volume but can also lower lead quality if the signal Meta optimizes against is a top-of-funnel form fill rather than a qualified lead. You may want to confirm the conversion event reflects a quality lead before opening targeting up, then test Advantage+ Audience against the current setup and watch downstream lead quality, not just lead count.

investigate.intro: Identify ad sets pinned to a narrow saved audience and check whether the account has the conversion signal that would make broad targeting worth testing.

meta_ads_manager.label: Meta Ads Manager
steps: Open Ads Manager and review active prospecting ad sets, noting which use Advantage+ Audience and which are pinned to a narrow saved or detailed-targeting audience.
For ad sets pinned narrow, note the audience size estimate and whether a clear performance reason for the narrow setup is documented.
Flag ad sets where delivery is constrained without a documented reason as candidates for a broad-targeting test.

meta_events_manager.label: Events Manager
steps: Open Events Manager and review the dataset feeding the account.
Confirm the conversion event is firing steadily and that it is being received through both the pixel and the Conversions API.
A steady, deduplicated conversion signal is the condition that makes broad targeting worth testing. A sparse or browser-only signal weakens the case for opening targeting up.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per prospecting ad set pinned to a narrow audience, whether a clear performance reason is documented and whether the account sends a steady conversion signal through the pixel and the Conversions API. An ad set kept narrow for a documented reason, or on an account without a reliable signal, passes. A narrow ad set with no documented reason on an account with a steady signal is flagged as a candidate for a broad-targeting test, recorded with the ad set name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record, for each narrow prospecting ad set above, whether a performance reason is documented and whether a steady conversion signal is reaching Meta to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that some of {{site_name}}'s prospecting ad sets are pinned to a narrow saved audience with Advantage+ Audience turned off, on an account already sending a steady conversion signal through the pixel and the Conversions API. A narrow audience caps the pool Meta can draw from, so the system cannot find converters it is not allowed to reach, which can hold delivery below where it could perform. {{voice_recommend}} testing Advantage+ Audience against the current setup on these ad sets, comparing cost per result over a full learning cycle before deciding. Where the offer is niche or the signal is weak, keeping the defined audience may still be the better call.
